# rivanna.dev

> effortless GPU computing on UVA's Rivanna cluster

rv is a CLI tool for running GPU jobs on UVA's Rivanna HPC cluster. no SLURM scripts, no partition guessing, one command to submit.

## docs

- [getting started](https://rivanna.dev/docs/getting-started.md): install rv, set up SSH, run your first job
- [commands](https://rivanna.dev/docs/commands.md): full reference for all 15 rv commands
- [dependencies & environment](https://rivanna.dev/docs/dependencies.md): venv management, dependency install, torchrun, troubleshooting
- [smart allocator](https://rivanna.dev/docs/allocator.md): how fan-out strategy and backfill detection work
- [configuration](https://rivanna.dev/docs/configuration.md): config file reference (~/.rv/config.toml)
- [guides](https://rivanna.dev/docs/gpu-training.md): tips, gotchas, queue times, GPU training best practices, troubleshooting
- [changelog](https://rivanna.dev/docs/changelog.md): version history and release notes

## dependencies & environment (critical for agents)

rv auto-manages a persistent Python venv per project per branch at
`/scratch/{user}/.rv/envs/{project}/{branch}/`. it installs from
`requirements.txt` or `pyproject.toml` using `uv pip install`. the venv is
activated in every job — `python`, `torchrun`, `pip` all resolve to the venv,
not the system (which is Python 3.6 and must never be used).

**use relative paths for scripts.** your command runs from within a snapshot of
your synced project. `rv run torchrun train.py` is correct.
`rv run torchrun /scratch/.../train.py` is WRONG — it bypasses the workspace
and may use system binaries instead of the venv's, causing ModuleNotFoundError.
absolute paths are fine for DATA (datasets, model weights on /scratch/).

**do not** use `uv sync`, `uv run`, `conda`, or create manual venvs — these
conflict with rv's venv. do not `pip install` into the system Python via
`rv exec`. do not use `rv exec` for GPU work (it runs on the login node).

**two-phase install:** most packages install on the login node. CUDA-dependent
packages (flash-attn, auto-gptq) are deferred to the compute node where GPU +
gcc are available. this happens transparently on first job run.

**torchrun** comes from the venv (installed with torch). rv auto-injects
`--master-port` to prevent port collisions. for multi-node, rv also handles
`--nnodes`, `--node-rank`, `--master-addr` via srun.

**verify your env** before expensive runs:
```
rv run --mig python -c "import sys; print(sys.executable); import torch; print(torch.cuda.is_available())"
```
sys.executable should show `/scratch/.../.rv/envs/.../bin/python`, not `/usr/bin/python3`.

**GLIBCXX errors:** if you see `GLIBCXX_3.4.29 not found`, run:
`rv env set LD_LIBRARY_PATH /apps/software/standard/core/gcc/14.2.0/lib64`

for extra packages beyond your deps file, add `pip install foo` in your script
(installs into rv's active venv, persists). or better: add them to your
requirements.txt.

see [dependencies & environment](https://rivanna.dev/docs/dependencies.md) for
full details.
